name: Buskerud (Norway, 1966–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms

description: A white field charged with a stylised standing bear figure derived from a medieval Sigdal church carving.
history: Granted to Buskerud county on 30 December 1966. The arms and flag are based on a medieval relief from Sigdal Church depicting a bear associated with Saint Olav. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Akershus and Ostfold into the new Viken county; Buskerud was re-established on 1 January 2024 following Viken's dissolution.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a depiction of Saint Olav's silver bear of Sigdal, derived from a medieval church carving in the county
the legendary bear of Saint Olav, taken from a medieval relief in Sigdal Church; symbolises the county's heritage
